title: Famous Buildings Go Dark for Earth Hour
summary: On Saturday night, famous buildings like the Eiffel Tower in Paris and Sagrada Família in Barcelona turned off their lights for one hour at 8:30 PM local time. This event is called Earth Hour, and it happens every year to help people think about protecting our planet. It started in Sydney, Australia, in 2007, and is organized by the World Wide Fund for Nature. During Earth Hour, people are encouraged to switch off their lights for 60 minutes to save energy and show they care about the environment. The event brings attention to issues like climate change and encourages everyone to take small actions to help Earth.

background_read: Earth Hour is a global event that happens once a year to remind people about taking care of our planet. It encourages everyone to turn off non-essential lights for one hour to save energy and show support for environmental protection. The event started in 2007 in Sydney and has grown to include millions of people and landmarks around the world. It helps teach us about issues like climate change and how small actions can make a big difference.
Article_Structure: WHO: Famous buildings like the Eiffel Tower and Sagrada Família, organized by the World Wide Fund for Nature.
WHAT: They turned off their lights for one hour for Earth Hour to raise environmental awareness.
WHERE: In cities like Paris and Barcelona, and it started in Sydney, Australia.
WHY: To encourage people to save energy and think about protecting the planet.

perspectives.description: Earth Hour helps people learn about and care for the environment by taking a simple action.
